heading: Waivers
label: 3030c–3 Waivers
notes: Codification, which directed the addition of this section at the end of Part A of title III ( et seq.), was executed by adding this section at the end of Part A of title III of the Older Americans Act of 1965 to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 106–501, § 31042 U.S.C. 3021

children.text: approval of the State legislature has been obtained or is not required with respect to the proposal for which waiver is sought;
the State agency has collaborated with the area agencies on aging in the State and other organizations that would be affected with respect to the proposal for which waiver is sought;

the proposal has been made available for public review and comment, including the opportunity for a public hearing upon request, within the State (and a summary of all of the comments received has been included in the application); and

the State agency has given adequate consideration to the probable positive and negative consequences of approval of the waiver application, and the probable benefits for older individuals can reasonably be expected to outweigh any negative consequences, or particular circumstances in the State otherwise justify the waiver.

any provision of sections 3025, 3026, and 3027 of this title requiring statewide uniformity of programs carried out under this subchapter, to the extent necessary to permit demonstrations, in limited areas of a State, of innovative approaches to assist older individuals;

any area plan requirement described in if granting the waiver will promote innovations or improve service delivery and will not diminish services already provided under this chapter;section 3026(a) of this title

any State plan requirement described in if granting the waiver will promote innovations or improve service delivery and will not diminish services already provided under this chapter;section 3027(a) of this title
any restriction under paragraph (5) of , on the amount that may be transferred between programs carried out under part B and part C; andsection 3028(b) of this title

the requirement of that certain amounts of a State allotment be used for the provision of services, with respect to a State that reduces expenditures under the State plan of the State (but only to the extent that the non-Federal share of the expenditures is not reduced below any minimum specified in or any other provision of this subchapter).section 3029(c) of this titlesection 3024(d) of this title

children.heading: Duration of waiver
children.label: c Duration of waiver
children.text: The application by a State agency for a waiver under this section shall include a recommendation as to the duration of the waiver (not to exceed the duration of the State plan of the State). The Assistant Secretary, in granting such a waiver, shall specify the duration of the waiver, which may be the duration recommended by the State agency or such shorter time period as the Assistant Secretary finds to be appropriate.

children.heading: Reports to Secretary
children.label: d Reports to Secretary
children.text: With respect to each waiver granted under this section, not later than 1 year after the expiration of such waiver, and at any time during the waiver period that the Assistant Secretary may require, the State agency shall prepare and submit to the Assistant Secretary a report evaluating the impact of the waiver on the operation and effectiveness of programs and services provided under this subchapter.
